title: GitHub Actions OIDC trust to a cloud account — three-axis sub claim, UNC6426 mitigation
description: A platform engineer wires GitHub Actions to assume a role in AWS / Azure / GCP via OIDC federation. The cloud-side trust policy must validate the GitHub-issued token's `sub` claim against a three-axis shape (`repo:{org}/{repo}:job_workflow_ref:{org}/{repo}/.github/workflows/{workflow}@{ref}:environment:{environment}`). UNC6426 (March 2026, ~500 vulnerable role ARNs across 275 AWS accounts per CSA Labs / Unit 42 OH-MY-DC analyses) weaponized exactly the wildcard `repo:{org}/{repo}:*` shape that Pulumi's GitHub provider accepts as default. Hulumi's `OrgFoundation` (M2) ships the safe template; the M3 `G_OIDC_1` policy declaratively rejects `StringLike` / wildcard `sub` conditions on AWS / Azure / GCP trust policies.
actors: AI Coding Agent (trusted, authoring IaC via Claude Code)
External Attacker controlling a fork or a separate repo in the same org
Compromised third-party Action (semi-trusted, via supply-chain compromise)
assets: Cloud-side IAM role assumed by the workflow
`sub` claim shape in the cloud trust policy
Production cloud resources downstream of the assumed role

stride.name: OIDC sub-claim wildcard impersonation (UNC6426)
stride.description: An attacker who can run any workflow in the same org (e.g. via a fork or a dev repo) requests an OIDC token; the cloud trust policy uses `StringLike` on `sub` with wildcard `repo:{org}/{repo}:*`; the attacker's token is accepted and the role is assumed. Mitigation: Hulumi's `G_OIDC_1` policy rejects `StringLike` and any `*` in the `sub` axis on AWS / Azure / GCP at preview-time; `OrgFoundation` ships the three-axis safe default for the GitHub-side `ActionsOrganizationOidcSubjectClaimCustomizationTemplate`.

stride.name: Trust policy tampering via console
stride.description: An admin clicks in the AWS / Azure / GCP console and broadens the OIDC trust policy's `sub` condition for a one-off use case, leaving the broadened condition in place after the use case closes. Drift detection (M4 webhook fallback adapter) cannot see cloud-side changes; the cloud-side IaC tag (`hulumi:iac-role=true`) carries forward. Mitigation: `G_OIDC_1` re-evaluates the trust policy on every `pulumi up`; cloud-side audit log cross-check against the Hulumi-managed expected shape.

stride.name: Information disclosure via over-scoped role
stride.description: The role assumed by the GitHub Actions workflow has broader permissions than the workflow needs (e.g. read-only on a PR-validation workflow but the role allows writes). A pwn-request-style exploit (compromised PR diff) gains write capability beyond the workflow author's intent.

stride.name: Privilege escalation via job_workflow_ref spoofing
stride.description: An attacker triggers a reusable workflow in a sibling repo whose `job_workflow_ref` matches what the trust policy permits, but the calling repo is not the intended one. Without an `environment` axis, the trust policy cannot distinguish prod-deploy from non-prod-deploy callers. Mitigation: include `environment` in the `sub` axis; require explicit `environment: prod` on prod-deploy workflows; AWS `StringEquals` (not `StringLike`) on the full three-axis string.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/baseline.github.OrgFoundation
recommendedComponents.rationale: Ships the GitHub-side `ActionsOrganizationOidcSubjectClaimCustomizationTemplate` with the three-axis safe default. Runtime rejects custom templates containing `*`.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/policies.github.G_OIDC_1
recommendedComponents.rationale: Declarative CrossGuard rule rejecting `StringLike` / wildcard `sub` conditions on AWS / Azure / GCP trust policies at preview-time. Covers AWS IAM, Azure Workload Identity Federation, and GCP Workload Identity Federation.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/baseline.aws.AccountFoundation
recommendedComponents.rationale: Provides the cloud-side IAM role tagged `hulumi:iac-role=true` so audit logs distinguish IaC-originated assumes from console break-glass.
